issue.title: Customer update leaks restricted checkout incident data

patch.diff:
diff --git a/src/checkout_status/update_builder.py b/src/checkout_status/update_builder.py
index c49c718..6c7c944 100644
--- a/src/checkout_status/update_builder.py
+++ b/src/checkout_status/update_builder.py
@@ -1,5 +1,19 @@
 """Build customer-facing checkout incident updates."""
 
+RESTRICTED_FIELDS = ('customer_email', 'order_id', 'request_payload', 'access_token', 'stack_trace')
+ALLOWED_DIAGNOSTICS = ('error_code',)
+
+
+def redact_incident_log(raw_log):
+    """Return incident details that are safe for customer status updates."""
+    diagnostics = {}
+    for key, value in raw_log.items():
+        if key in RESTRICTED_FIELDS:
+            diagnostics[key] = "[redacted]"
+        elif key in ALLOWED_DIAGNOSTICS:
+            diagnostics[key] = value
+    return diagnostics
+
 
 def build_customer_update(incident):
     """Return the update payload sent to customers."""
@@ -7,5 +21,6 @@ def build_customer_update(incident):
         "service": incident["service"],
         "status": incident["status"],
         "summary": incident["summary"],
-        "raw_log": incident["raw_log"],
+        "diagnostics": redact_incident_log(incident.get("raw_log", {})),
+        "next_update": incident.get("next_update", "15 minutes"),
     }

counterfactuals.action: redact restricted fields and keep allowed diagnostics

findings.claim: Customer updates cannot contain customer_email, order_id, request_payload, access_token, stack_trace, raw_log.
The regression tests check exact restricted values and required context.
The implementation copies a restricted raw_log field into the external update.

collective_goals.statement: Give customers useful status context without publishing internal logs.
Keep non-sensitive diagnostics that help responders identify the failure mode.

individual_goals.statement: Do not expose customer identifiers, tokens, payloads, or traces.

norms.target: external update with safe operational context

hyperbase_contract.purpose: make docs, tests, and code claims queryable as structured propositions

projection_rules.demo_use: sequence security review before external update before public follow-up
shows COLORE definitions can compile into goal-directed composition rules
projection_rules.gloss: Conservative Definitional Extension A person has a grandchild if their child has a child.

projection_rules.demo_use: shows an ontology-derived relation can stay queryable without guessing
projection_rules.gloss: Conservative Definitional Extension For children who have the same parent, they are siblings.
